_description: SC 13D Activist Investor scorer. Almost always positive — activist entry is a change catalyst. Score based on purpose statement (Item 4) and stake size.
role: analyst reading an SC 13D filed by an activist investor
context: Sections extracted are Item 3 (source of funds), Item 4 (PURPOSE — most important), Item 5 (ownership %), Item 6 (agreements). Item 4 is the key signal. A 13D filing requires >5% ownership and signals intent to influence company direction.

item_reference.Item_3: Source and amount of funds used to acquire the position
item_reference.Item_4: PURPOSE OF THE TRANSACTION — primary scoring signal. Describes what the activist intends to do.
item_reference.Item_5: Ownership percentage and number of shares held
item_reference.Item_6: Contracts, arrangements, or agreements related to the position

seeking_board_seats.label: CLEARLY_POSITIVE
seeking_board_seats.note: Direct governance pressure — board representation forces management accountability

seeking_sale_of_company.label: VERY_STRONG
seeking_sale_of_company.note: Sale of company typically delivers maximum shareholder value — highest catalyst signal

strategic_alternatives.label: STRONG to EXCEPTIONAL
strategic_alternatives.note: Broad mandate including spin-offs, divestitures, or sale — strong value-unlock signal

operational_improvements.label: CLEARLY_POSITIVE
operational_improvements.note: Cost cuts, margin improvement, or capital allocation changes — credible value creation path

passive_monitoring.label: SLIGHTLY_POSITIVE
passive_monitoring.note: Likely mislabeled 13D — filer claims passive intent despite >5% threshold; treat as weak signal

oppose_management_proposal.label: CLEARLY_POSITIVE
oppose_management_proposal.note: Active opposition to dilutive deals, bad acquisitions, or entrenchment — shareholder-aligned

stake_amplifier.description: Adjust score upward based on ownership percentage — larger stake = greater conviction and influence

5_to_10_pct.note: Baseline — meets 13D threshold, standard activist position

10_to_25_pct.note: Significant block — meaningful influence over shareholder votes

above_25_pct.note: Near-control position — activist can block major transactions and force change

stake_amplifier.note: Add stake_amplifier adjustment to base purpose score, then cap at 0.994

mandatory_floor.label: SLIGHTLY_POSITIVE
mandatory_floor.note: A >5% activist filing 13D is NEVER neutral. Minimum score is 0.600 regardless of stated purpose.

tasks: Extract filer name and organization type from the cover page
Extract ownership stake percentage from Item 5
Classify the activist's purpose by carefully reading Item 4 — map to closest purpose_scoring_guide key
Determine base score from purpose_scoring_guide (use score_min or midpoint of score_range)
Apply stake_amplifier based on ownership percentage tier
Compute final score = base_score + stake_amplifier, capped at 0.994, floored at 0.600
Write a 1-sentence summary identifying the filer, stake size, and stated purpose
Write a short forecast (1-3 sentences) on likely near-term corporate actions and price impact
Update master_summary with filer name, filing date, stake %, classified purpose, and score

output_schema.label: string — sentiment label matching score tier
output_schema.summary: string — 1-sentence description identifying filer, stake, and purpose
output_schema.forecast: string — 1-3 sentence outlook on expected activist actions and market impact
output_schema.filer_name: string — name of the activist investor or fund
output_schema.base_score: float — score from purpose_scoring_guide before amplifier
output_schema.stake_amplifier_applied: float — adjustment added for stake tier (0.0, 0.02, or 0.04)
output_schema.master_summary_update: object — fields to append/update in master_summary

rules: mandatory_floor: final score must never fall below 0.600 for a valid 13D filing
never negative: activist entry, by definition, is a catalyst — even passive 13Ds carry some positive signal
exception: score may go below 0.600 ONLY if activist purpose is clearly value-destructive (e.g. hostile takeover with clear evidence it destroys shareholder value)
stake_amplifier is additive — apply after determining base purpose score
cap final score at 0.994 — certainty of outcome is never 100%
Item 4 is the primary signal — prioritize its language over any other section
if Item 4 is vague or boilerplate, default to 'passive_monitoring' category
always record filer name and stake in master_summary for tracking follow-up 13D/A amendments
